name: Physics Channel - Stage 3 - Social Media Upload

parameters.jsCode:
return [{ json: {
  job_id: "26",
  title: "The Higgs Field: Unveiling Mass",
  topic: "Higgs Boson",
  narration: "The Higgs boson is a quantum ripple in a universal field. This field, the Higgs field, interacts with particles, giving them mass. At the LHC, protons collide, creating fleeting Higgs bosons. Detectors capture their decay products, reconstructing the invisible particle's signature.",
  description: "The Higgs boson is a quantum ripple in a universal field. This field, the Higgs field, interacts with particles, giving them mass. At the LHC, protons collide, creating fleeting Higgs bosons. Detectors capture their decay products, reconstructing the invisible particle's signature.",
  hashtags: "#HiggsBoson #ParticlePhysics #CERN #LHC #QuantumField",
  final_video_path: "/data/ffmpeg/output/26_final.mp4",
  output_filename: "26_final.mp4",
  drive_file_id: "1GzzTD6VFDp3_zTMtKxeC28eg-tEBJz-D",
  drive_video_url: "https://drive.google.com/uc?export=download&id=1GzzTD6VFDp3_zTMtKxeC28eg-tEBJz-D",
  sheet_row: 29,
  callback_url: null
}}];

const data = $input.first().json;
const title = data.title || 'Physics Discovery';
const topic = data.topic || title;
const narration = data.narration || data.description || topic;

const prompt = `You are the content writer for an advanced physics brand channel — authoritative, intellectually sharp, and engaging. Think PBS Space Time, Veritasium, or Scientific American — but for social media.

Video topic: "${title}"

Video context (STRICTLY USE THIS STORY AS THE FACTUAL CORE):
"${narration}"

Write two platform posts based on the context above. Both posts must:
- Be descriptive and engaging — Target exactly 230-260 words per post across 4 to 5 paragraphs.
- NEVER use: "I", "we", "in this video", "the narrator".
- NEVER use markdown formatting: no ** bold **, no * italic *, no __ underline __.
- NEVER use special characters: no /, no $, no %, no &, no @, no ^, no ~, no |.
- NEVER use bullet points, dashes, or numbered lists inside the caption body.
- Use plain flowing prose only. Clean readable text with no symbols.
- Each paragraph should be 2-4 sentences. Separate paragraphs with a blank line.

FACEBOOK POST (230-260 words, 4-5 paragraphs):
- Paragraph 1: Open with a striking physical fact or jaw-dropping hook from the narration.
- Paragraph 2: Explain the core mechanism or phenomenon in accessible but precise language.
- Paragraph 3: Deepen the story — connect it to a broader physical principle or real-world consequence.
- Paragraph 4: Expand on why this matters — technology, discovery, or shift in understanding.
- Paragraph 5 (optional): End with a sharp insight or open question to spark comments.

LINKEDIN POST (230-260 words, 4-5 paragraphs):
- Paragraph 1: Open with the professional/scientific core insight — authoritative and confident.
- Paragraph 2: Explain the physics mechanism clearly and precisely.
- Paragraph 3: Connect to the broader scientific or technological significance.
- Paragraph 4: Discuss real-world implications — industry, research, or human understanding.
- Paragraph 5: Close with a bold declarative statement about what this means for the future of science.

HASHTAGS (20 tags):
- 5 broad tags (#physics #science #stem etc.)
- 15 specific tags matching the context

YOUTUBE SHORTS DESCRIPTION (60-90 words):
- One punchy hook sentence.
- Interesting explanation building on the narration.
- 3-5 hashtags at end.

Return ONLY raw JSON:
{
  "facebook_caption": "...",
  "linkedin_caption": "...",
  "youtube_caption": "...",
  "hashtags": "#tag1 #tag2 ..."
}`;

return [{ json: {
  ...data,
  gemini_caption_request: {
    contents: [{ parts: [{ text: prompt }] }],
    generationConfig: {
      maxOutputTokens: 2048,
      temperature: 0.75,
      responseMimeType: 'application/json'
    }
  }
}}];

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ job_id: $json.job_id, fb_post_url: $json.fb_post_url, fb_status: $json.fb_status, li_post_url: $json.li_post_url, li_status: $json.li_status, status: 'stage3_complete', publish_time_sec: $json.publish_time_sec }) }}
